text: i used to count the coins in her change purse while she drove. i can't remember when i stopped doing that.
finding an old lipstick in a coat pocket that still smelled like her.
she kept every receipt. i found a stack of them years later and couldn't throw them away.
the hum was always there. i stopped noticing it until it stopped.
staying in bed too long, the blanket too warm, missing things i should have been at.
the smell of the tape loading. we watched the same movie every christmas.
opening a freezer in a store i can't remember anymore. just the cold air on my face.
the smell of that waiting room stays with me even though i've tried to forget it.
we used to sit in the stairwell and talk. i can't remember what we talked about.
there was a creek behind the house. it's a parking lot now.
the dog would come in from the rain and shake water everywhere. she's been gone a long time.
the summer it flooded. the smell never fully left.
someone always cried when a balloon popped at parties. i can't remember whose birthday it was.
lighting candles at a table i can't picture anymore.
the last summer at that pool. we didn't know it was the last time.

scent: ball pit at a party place
text: i remember going but not who was there. just the smell and the noise.
the summer reading program. i don't remember winning or losing. just going.
we went every day after school. i don't know what happened to that place.
i gave it away and immediately regretted it. i can't remember where it went.
my grandmother's couch. gone along with the house.
waking up sick and having it wrapped around me. i don't know what happened to it.
i used to listen to music on the bus. i can't remember what i was listening to.

scent: rain poncho from a theme park
text: there are photos from that trip but i can't remember actually being there.

scent: rubber ducky from a dollar store
text: i can picture it but not the context. not the hands that gave it to me.
running to catch the ice cream truck. i can't remember who i was running with.
it was in the backyard of a house i can't fully picture anymore.
the last game of the season. i didn't know it was the last season.
walking home alone. the city felt empty in a way that used to feel okay.

scent: someone else's perfume in the dark
text: i can't place who it was. just the feeling of someone nearby.
pressing my face against the cold window of a moving car at night. i don't know where we were going.
standing at the open freezer not looking for anything. just standing.
i forgot about it. smoke alarm went off. everyone was home.
the coffee maker would start automatically. even after.

scent: trash bins on a hot day
text: forgetting to bring them in. it felt like more than forgetting.
there were parties at their house every summer. we stopped being invited and i never asked why.

scent: outdoor carpet on a front step
text: i can picture the mat but not the door it was in front of.
they left it and i kept it too long before saying anything.
my grandfather smoked. i didn't know that smell was a gift.

scent: the back of a car seat
text: leaning against someone's shoulder in the back seat. i can't remember who.
the phone always charged by the same outlet. in the house i grew up in.
a mix CD someone made for me. i don't know where it is.
missing something important. i still dream about this one.
